entities.name: Abraxas
entities.description: A gnostic God/deity, shown as a snake-footed hybrid. His name has a numeric value of 365.

entities.name: The King (Gold)
entities.description: In alchemical processes, the gold or king can be dissolved in water and handed to Vulcan.

entities.name: The Queen
entities.description: In alchemical processes, the queen is fecundated by a greasy python to conceive children.

entities.name: Blue Rose
entities.description: The 'flower of wisdom'; signifies a moist state of matter and is a modification of saturnine black.

attributes.significance: moist state of matter, high spirituality, arcane knowledge

entities.name: Proteus
entities.description: An eternally wandering watery man from Greek mythology, identified with 'our Mercury' by Heinrich Khunrath.

entities.name: Sothis (Sirius)
entities.description: A fixed star associated with Isis and the Egyptian time-wheel.

entities.name: Abraxas Gemstones
entities.description: Gemstones featuring the magical name of Abraxas, often surrounded by the Ouroboros.

summary: This passage explores the multifaceted symbolism of the serpent and Ouroboros in alchemy, gnosticism, and Egyptian cosmology. It details alchemical transformations involving the 'king' and 'queen', the symbolic meaning of colored roses, and the cosmic significance of Abraxas and the Egyptian time-wheel.
